refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral, specifying 'support teleoperation' and 'latency compatible with safe remote operation' without prescribing how to achieve it. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is a clear functional requirement related to safety, not a solution. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency level supports safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Use a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) for teleoperation data
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Segment teleoperation data into prioritized streams to ensure critical commands have minimal latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and data processing modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or hardware, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive algorithms are not selected here, which could help but add complexity
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for communication quality assurance (e.g., QoS mechanisms) to further ensure latency targets under varying network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data input and output for processing and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it is the main system architecture module designed for data processing; minimal diffusion expected as buffering and processing are centralized here.
Vehicle module may handle some data but primarily focused on physical vehicle functions; real-time processing would diffuse outside.
Handles remote control tasks but not core data processing; partial containment but significant diffusion.
Focused on safety alerts and monitoring, not on data processing; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it centralizes processing logic and data flow management, minimizing diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if remote commands require real-time processing.
Integration complexity with Vehicle module if some data processing is embedded in vehicle systems.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the teleoperation latency requirement (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if remote commands require real-time processing.
Integration complexity with Vehicle module if some data processing is embedded in vehicle systems.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well centralized within this IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the hypothesis that NGHE Solution is the appropriate module to contain the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end communication latency within the NGHE Solution module and its interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit scope and interfaces defined to support the FR.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the interface and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to mitigate diffusion risk related to real-time processing of remote commands.
Assess and define clear boundaries with the Vehicle module to avoid embedding critical data processing that could increase diffusion and complicate integration.
Include explicit verification steps in the safety plan to measure latency performance within the NGHE Solution module to ensure compliance with the acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and low diffusion, supporting a well-centralized implementation. Although some diffusion risks and integration complexities are noted, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the safety-critical nature is addressed by including explicit verification steps in the safety plan. Therefore, no further sub-FRs derivation is required now.

revise.why: To clarify and document interfaces and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and other modules (Teleoperation and Vehicle) to mitigate diffusion risks and integration complexity, and to include explicit verification steps in the safety plan for latency performance.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR with respect to latency requirements.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for the DP implementation.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module regarding real-time processing of remote commands.
Integration complexity with Vehicle module if data processing is embedded there.
evidence_needed: Verification data measuring end-to-end communication latency within NGHE Solution module and its interfaces under agreed network conditions.
Documentation clarifying interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle modules.
